result:
I’ve been thinking a lot about hidden frequencies lately—the little oscillations of life that hum beneath what we notice. They’re not metaphorical in the whimsical sense (though they could be); I mean the literal rhythms that shape everything around us.

Think of a heart. We take the beat for granted because it never steps into the spotlight unless something goes wrong. But try listening closely—not just to the lub-dub, but to how it changes when you sprint up a stairwell, or when an anxious thought sneaks in. The heart pays attention to the world even when the conscious mind does not. It calls for aid, accommodates, adapts. It remembers stress, holds adrenaline like a note sustained, and eventually lets go. Inside that chest, there’s a symphony of responsive adjustments—your own private orchestra.

Invisible rhythms span wider than our bodies. Trees bend toward the sun in smooth arcs, the ocean repeats its tidal breaths, the moon sways us with gravity we rarely feel. Yet somehow, we’re so enthralled by the fast world—notifications, deadlines, the next new headline—that we forget about the slow music beneath our feet.

I find oddly serene comfort in remembering these quiet metronomes. The universe is messy, but it is not unpatterned. Order reverberates even through apparent chaos; sometimes it just takes patience to hear the melody. Tune your attention, and a skylark’s morning trill becomes a gateway into dawn, the hiss of traffic a reminder of countless stories in motion, each with their own tempo.

There’s no grand lesson here—only a simple invitation: today, pick one small rhythm that lives around you. Listen for it. See how it changes. Let it anchor you to the deeper cadence of being alive.
